articles.title: ‘Arman No!!’ DJ Breaks Down Arman Loss vs Colby
The Truth About Conor McGregor's Road Back
articles.description: Conor McGregor suffered another devastating knee injury, but Chael Sonnen believes fans are missing the bigger picture. Instead of debating whether Conor can beat Max Holloway or questioning if he'll ever return, Chael explains why now is the time to support one of the biggest stars the sport has ever seen. From rehab and recovery to motivation and mindset, there's an order to every comeback—and Conor is only on the first step. Chael also discusses Conor's plan to run it back with Max Holloway, why history with Nate Diaz matters, and why nobody should be rushing to write off "The Notorious" just yet. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #UFCReturn #TheNotorious #CombatSportsBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The UFC's Next Superstar
articles.description: Tommy McMillen didn't just win—he announced himself. After an unforgettable performance, Chael Sonnen explains why Tommy has suddenly become one of the hottest names in MMA. From his three-time state wrestling championships to his unbelievable toughness and the incredible story of wanting to fight professionally as an eight-year-old, this isn't just another prospect. Chael believes Tommy McMillen has everything it takes to become the UFC's next breakout star—and after hearing his story, it's easy to understand why. #UFC #TommyMcMillen #MMA #ChaelSonnen #Prospect #UFCProspects #CombatSports Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Arman Tsarukyan did NOT Start a Fight!
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan may have lost the wrestling match, but he won Chael Sonnen's respect. After facing Colby Covington at Real American Freestyle, Arman didn't make excuses, start an argument, or create another viral moment. Instead, he showed something Chael didn't expect: the mindset of a true competitor. Chael explains why Tsarukyan's performance, his relentless effort until the final whistle, and his reaction after the match convinced him that Arman is even closer to becoming a UFC champion than before. Plus, why Kurt Angle's comments about Arman's wrestling might not be as crazy as they sound. #UFC #ArmanTsarukyan #ChaelSonnen #Wrestling #MMA #ColbyCovington #RealAmericanFreestyle #IslamMakhachev #CombatSports Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Did Du Plessis Earn Another Title Shot?
articles.description: Did Dricus Du Plessis do enough to earn another title shot? Chael Sonnen breaks down the middleweight title picture after DDP's win over Kamaru Usman and explains why the situation is far more complicated than fans realize. He also reveals new information from a conversation with Nassourdine Imavov, who says he hasn't turned down any fights because the UFC has never offered him one. That changes everything. With Khamzat Chimaev, Sean Strickland, Imavov, and DDP all in the mix, who really deserves the next title shot? And if DDP doesn't get it, Chael has one fight in mind that he absolutely wants to see. articles.title: Tommy McMillen: “I’m Coming for the UFC Top 15!” | INTERVIEW
articles.description: Michael Bisping and Paul Felder sit down with rising UFC featherweight Tommy “The Gun” McMillen following his incredible TKO victory over Alberto Montes at UFC Oklahoma City. McMillen breaks down the relentless performance that saw him land a UFC three-round record of 252 significant strikes, responds to everyone who predicted he would be “fraud checked” and explains why he believes he can defeat anyone in the featherweight top 15. Tommy also discusses his rapid rise through the UFC, receiving a message from Hunter Campbell after the fight, learning to control his wild side and his plans to remain active as he begins his pursuit of a UFC title shot. Subscribe for more exclusive interviews, reactions and fight breakdowns from UFC Hall of Famer Michael Bisping. #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews

articles.title: Kamaru Usman Should Stay at Middleweight
articles.description: Kamaru Usman may have just found the answer to the next chapter of his career. Chael Sonnen explains why he's still intrigued by a potential Islam Makhachev fight, why retirement has always been about purpose—not ability—and why Usman's performance against Dricus Du Plessis convinced him of something unexpected. Instead of returning to welterweight, Chael believes Usman should make a permanent move to middleweight. After competing well against Khamzat Chimaev on short notice and pushing DDP over five rounds, what could Usman do if he fully committed to 185 pounds? Sometimes the best career move isn't chasing another title—it's changing divisions. articles.title: Ben Askren's Final Match Was Bigger Than Wrestling
articles.description: One year ago, Chael Sonnen was preparing to attend Ben Askren's funeral. Now, Ben Askren just walked out in front of his hometown crowd for one final wrestling match. Chael recaps an unforgettable night at Real American Freestyle in Milwaukee, from an electric crowd to Ben's emotional return after surviving four medical deaths, a double lung transplant, and an unimaginable recovery. He also explains why Belal Mohammed deserves tremendous credit for making the moment possible, and why Ben's toughest battle wasn't on the mat—it was simply standing in front of the fans one last time to say thank you. Some nights are bigger than wins and losses. This was one of them. articles.title: Kamaru Usman Is MMA's Toughest Matchup
articles.description: Dricus Du Plessis had far more to prove than most fans realized. Coming off the biggest loss of his career, DDP stepped into a dangerous matchup against former welterweight champion Kamaru Usman. Chael explains why this wasn't just another main event—it was a test of character. Can you beat Kamaru standing? Wrestling? Conditioning? History says no. Chael breaks down why Usman presents one of the toughest stylistic puzzles in MMA, the controversial timeout moment in the fifth round, and why DDP's sportsmanship may have said even more than his performance. If anyone was still asking how good Dricus Du Plessis really is, Chael thinks they finally have their answer. articles.title: The Truth About Dustin Poirier’s Incident #ufc
articles.description: Chael Sonnen is standing behind Dustin Poirier. In this breakdown, Chael addresses the recent incident involving his longtime friend and coworker, making it clear that Dustin's response was exactly what it should have been. While the footage has generated plenty of discussion online, Chael believes the story goes beyond what viewers saw on camera. Instead, he focuses on the professionalism shown by the responding officer. According to Chael, the officer recognized who Poirier was, understood the situation, and chose to de-escalate instead of making it worse. In an environment where many people might have escalated the encounter—calling for backup, using force, or turning it into a much bigger incident—the officer remained calm and handled the moment with remarkable composure. Chael says that's the part of the story Dustin appreciates the most. Rather than focusing on himself, Poirier's concern has been how to properly thank the officer for treating everyone involved with respect. Chael believes that speaks volumes about both men and reminds viewers that true professionals often don't seek recognition for doing the right thing. Sometimes the best outcome isn't the most dramatic one—it's the one where cooler heads prevail. For more UFC analysis, Dustin Poirier updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #DustinPoirier #ChaelSonnen #MMA #CombatSports #UFCNews #FightNews #MixedMartialArts

articles.title: 🚨 KAMARU USMAN: Open To LHW “I’M A MONEYWEIGHT FIGHTER!” 💰👀
articles.description: Kamaru Usman says weight classes aren’t the priority anymore—the right opportunity is. The former UFC welterweight champion joked that he’s now a “moneyweight fighter,” saying he’ll compete wherever the biggest fight is available. 💬 “If it’s at welterweight, I’ll do it. If it’s at middleweight, I’ll do it. If it’s at light heavyweight… I might do it.” When asked if he’ll ever return to 170 pounds, Usman made it clear the door is still open if the right offer comes along. Which division do you want to see Kamaru Usman fight in next? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: KAMARU USMAN WANTS INTO THE UFC DOUBLE-CHAMP CLUB! 👀🔥
articles.description: Kamaru Usman believes he already belongs among the greatest fighters in UFC history—but there is still one exclusive club he wants to join: The UFC double-champion club. Usman says winning a second divisional title would cement his legacy as one of the sport’s all-time elite and give him something even many legendary fighters never achieved. Would becoming a two-division champion put Kamaru Usman in your all-time top five? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: DRICUS ROASTS KHAMZAT CHIMAEV! 🔥 Taekwondo "MORE VIOLENT" 👀
articles.description: Dricus Du Plessis took a shot at Khamzat Chimaev when asked how a young fan could convince their parents to let them train MMA. His answer? “Show them me and Khamzat [Chimaev]. They’ll let you fight immediately.” The UFC middleweight champion joked that their fight was so uneventful that parents would think taekwondo is more violent, adding: “If they don’t fall asleep, then he’s not allowed.” Does Dricus have a point or just sour grapes? 👇 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.description: The Craic is back, as Petesy Carroll and Chuck Mindenhall regale Ben Fowlkes with tales from Las Vegas.
